mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, causing institutional investors to reduce equity exposure and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth fails to predict negative 20-day returns during late-cycle periods, with a hit rate below 45% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate tightening expectations, raising discount rates and immediately depressing S&P 500 valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of high-inflation trigger months across a rolling 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls above 200,000 confirm robust economic growth and rising corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth above 200k fails to generate positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and rising personal consumption expenditures signals unsustainable consumer leverage that inevitably triggers a market correction and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence metric yields a positive rank IC over 20-day horizons in 70% of observed quarters, contradicting the negative return prediction.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling consumer prices indicates a supply-side recovery that boosts profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a hit rate above 55% during post-shock recovery periods over a 4-year walk-forward test.
